title: sub_GAME_7F02083C returns early for arg0==0 in port; retail applies the gun-hand aim-bone pose (arg0==0 block is dead in the port)
repro: During character aim-bone posing (g_aimBoneCallback invoked per bone at model.c:3701 and model.c:4012 with the bone index as arg0), the callback is called with arg0==0 for the gun-hand aim bone. Retail computes that bone's aim rotation (gunhand-based shoulder-angle selection); the port returns immediately, so bone 0 receives no aim pose. GE007 A/B flag not present.

history.note: Retail sub_GAME_7F02083C (VERSION_US glabel chr.c:4069) validity dispatch (ASM chr.c:4098-4106): beq arg0==2 -> proceed; beq arg0==3 -> proceed; beql arg0==1 -> proceed; bnezl arg0 -> return (L7F020D88). The bnezl is a branch-likely on arg0 != 0, so arg0==0 does NOT branch and falls through to the aim block (its delay slot lw ra is nullified). Retail therefore PROCEEDS for arg0==0 and runs the dedicated gun-hand block at ASM L7F020974: reads chr->hidden, and when !(hidden & 0x400) selects f14 = aimuprshoulder (offset 0x144) if chr->model->gunhand==0 else aimuplshoulder (0x140); when (hidden & 0x400) f14 = aimupback (0x148) clamped by D_80051D3C / D_80051D40. The live NONMATCHING C (chr.c:3814-3825) writes 'if (arg0==2 || arg0==3) {} else if (arg0==1) {} else { return; }' which returns for arg0==0, making the C's own arg0==0 aim block (chr.c:3877-3907, identical gun-hand selection) DEAD/unreachable. Player-visible: the gun-hand aim bone (index 0) is not posed by the port, so the character's gun-hand/arm does not track aim the way it does on retail. arg0==0 is a real input: g_aimBoneCallback is invoked with the bone index at model.c:3701/4012, and both the C and the retail ASM ship dedicated arg0==0 handling, confirming it is an expected value. The fix is to change the C early-return so arg0==0 falls through (e.g. 'else if (arg0 != 0) return;'), which un-deads the existing arg0==0 block. EVERYTHING ELSE in the function verified equivalent to the US ASM: the cheatIsActive(12) scale (arg0==0 -> 4.0, arg0==2/3 -> 2.5), the arg0==1/2/3 aim-angle selections (aimupback/aimuprshoulder/aimuplshoulder + aimsideback with the hidden&0x400 D_80051D30/34/38 clamps), the flinchcnt<0 skip and the arg0==2/3 vs arg0==1 flinch adjustments (sub_GAME_7F020794, D_80051D44/48, 15/360 scaling, hidden 0x1000/0x2000/0x4000/0x8000 -> f14/f12/roll), the all-zero + scale==1.0 early return, chrlvGetSubrotySideback, the f14 (neg or D_80051D4C - f14) and f12 (+ D_80051D50) adjustments, the player-matrix premultiply, the m[3] save/zero/restore, the f14==0 && roll==0 simple-Y-rot vs the full Y(f12-subroty + D_80051D54)/X(f14)/Z(roll)/Y(subroty) rotation chain, the scale!=1.0 matrix_scalar_multiply, and the camGetWorldToScreenMtxf premultiply. The !VERSION_US ASM variant (glabel chr.c:4451) is the same function with the same C body and the same divergence (the C is region-independent). Found by ASM-audit batch 10, queue ranks 116/119, region US.
